content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; `v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory; In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcript-dump or brittle fact-store failure modes.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; Memory repair targets duplicate notes/claims, contradictory active items, profile drift, stale task states, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage.; scope=Applies to the memory repair workflow described in this imported document section.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_constraint; Tier C rich note memory serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; Tier A core in-context memory should not include all claims, all notes, raw transcript, or long lists of facts.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> responsible_for; Memory worker or manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention in the current project architecture.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 10.2
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_constraint; Note path is used for important information that should not yet become hard facts, such as design direction, rationale/trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons/patterns, document digests, and contextual framing.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 semantic write paths
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_goal; The system goal is to have a memory layer for an AI agent that survives beyond a single session.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage; In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; Memory updates in the current project must not be append-only.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 9
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; The memory system must not convert every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> responsible_for; Storage layer is responsible for immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail in the current project architecture.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 10.3
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; The Note path should use a maturity lifecycle with stages fresh, stabilizing, mature, and consolidated.; scope=Applies to the architecture guidance in this imported document section.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to explain where a memory came from.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to support aging, archiving, and repairing memory.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture; Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic note-layer context, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes across memory objects.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Postgres; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Postgres; reason=Concrete technology explicitly listed; no existing candidates.
- mention=JSONB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=JSONB; reason=Concrete technical term explicitly listed; no existing candidates.
- mention=pgvector; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=pgvector; reason=Named extension explicitly listed; no existing candidates.
- mention=full-text indexes; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Full-text index; reason=Durable technical concept mentioned as a storage capability.
- mention=lineage tables; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Lineage table; reason=Reusable architectural concept explicitly listed.
- mention=Graph DB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Graph database; reason=Core technology discussed in section; no existing candidates.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=01. Architecture|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=01. Architecture agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB MARKDOWN Document title: 01. Architecture Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB Document lines: 547-558 ## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics. Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
